g.text: Channel Dimension Convention for Per-Channel Quantization

div.p: Matrix Multiplication: Y = X @ W.T For output element Y[b, c]:
Output Y[c] depends on row c of W. During quantized inference:
We need scale_c and zp_c per output channel.

text.tspan: # Each row divided by its own scale!
# All in one efficient tensor operation
# Each row divided by its own scale!
# All in one efficient tensor operation

g.text: Single tensor op, not a loop!

div.p: Pitfall 1: Wrong Dimension Using dim=1 (input features) instead of dim=0 → Scales don't match output channels → Wrong dequantization, garbage outputs
Pitfall 2: Shape Mismatch For Conv2d: scales must broadcast to [C_out, C_in, kH, kW] → Use .view(C_out, 1, 1, 1) not .view(C_out, 1)
Pitfall 3: Embedding Confusion Embeddings are lookups, not matmuls → Per-"channel" doesn't make sense → Usually kept in FP16 or FP32

g.text: Layer Type | Channel Dim | Scale Shape

div.p: Key Insight: Output dimension = channel dimension Because: Y[c] = f(W[c, :], X) Each output channel has its own row of weights, so each output channel needs its own scale.
